items.title: Out-of-control wildfire burning near Boston Bar, B.C. prompts evacuation for about 60 residences
items.bullet: Out-of-control wildfire burning near Boston Bar, B.C. prompts evacuation for about 60 residences (CBC British Columbia, Jul 5)
items.description: A tactical evacuation is underway in North Bend, B.C., where residents are being told to leave immediately because of the nearby Brunswick Creek wildfire that’s burning near Boston Bar in the province’s Fraser Canyon region.

items.title: Local impacts of pipeline to B.C. coast and Delta port expansion
items.bullet: Local impacts of pipeline to B.C. coast and Delta port expansion (CityNews Vancouver, Jul 4)
items.description: After Prime Minister Mark Carney and Premier David Eby announced the Canada – B.C. Cooperative Prosperity Agreement, Delta’s shipping port is now the centre of attention.

relatedSources.title: Why expansion of Delta container port is going hand-in-hand with Alberta pipeline proposal
relatedSources.reason: Same section; shared terms: coast, delta, expansion, pipeline.

relatedSources.title: North Coast oil tanker ban being upheld, Port of Vancouver expanding in multibillion-dollar B.C.-Ottawa deal
relatedSources.reason: Shared terms: agreement, coast, pipeline, premier.

items.title: How a First Nation in B.C. is hoping to restore wildfire-ravaged forests with seedlings
items.bullet: How a First Nation in B.C. is hoping to restore wildfire-ravaged forests with seedlings (CBC British Columbia, Jul 4)
items.description: The Osoyoos Forest Nursery located near Oliver, B.C, is owned by the Osoyoos Indian Band. The operation is the band's first green project and it is expected to produce millions of tree seedlings every year. Those seedlings will go towards restoring and reforesting areas hard hit by wildfires.

items.title: GVRDEU escalating strike action Saturday afternoon
items.bullet: GVRDEU escalating strike action Saturday afternoon (CityNews Vancouver, Jul 4)
items.description: As of 4 p.m., Saturday, the Greater Vancouver Regional District Employee Union is escalating their strike action.

items.title: Mock evacuation exercise to take place in Tatla Lake area
items.bullet: Mock evacuation exercise to take place in Tatla Lake area (CBC British Columbia, Jul 4)
items.description: Emergency crews will be training west of Williams Lake Saturday morning in a training exercise focused on evacuation notification.

items.title: Why expansion of Delta container port is going hand-in-hand with Alberta pipeline proposal
items.bullet: Why expansion of Delta container port is going hand-in-hand with Alberta pipeline proposal (CBC British Columbia, Jul 3)
items.description: The lack of a northern route for Alberta's proposed new pipeline to the B.C. coast may have grabbed the headlines during Thursday's announcement of an Ottawa-B.C. memorandum of understanding with more than $200 billion in investments. But analysts say the federal government's $10-billion commitment to a container port expansion in Delta, B.C., as part of the MOU means that Roberts Bank Terminal 2 (RBT2) is critical to the project, too.

relatedSources.title: Local impacts of pipeline to B.C. coast and Delta port expansion
relatedSources.reason: Same section; shared terms: coast, delta, expansion, pipeline.

relatedSources.title: Alberta pitches southern route for West Coast pipeline, with a price tag of $35B or more
Feds Announce Pipeline Steps, BC Advances Protected Areas
relatedSources.reason: Shared terms: alberta, coast, northern, pipeline.

items.title: To swat or not? These scientists suggest we rethink our fear toward insects
items.bullet: To swat or not? These scientists suggest we rethink our fear toward insects (CBC British Columbia, Jul 4)
items.description: When a bug lands, do you swat or step back? We are taught to fear insects, but by looking closer at how they help our environment, we can trade our reflexive panic for wonder and learn to share our outdoor spaces, scientists say.

items.title: These Osoyoos twins were both drafted by the Pittsburgh Penguins. Their parents say it's all about passion
items.bullet: These Osoyoos twins were both drafted by the Pittsburgh Penguins. Their parents say it's all about passion (CBC British Columbia, Jul 4)
items.description: Twins Markus and Liam Ruck of Osoyoos, B.C., were both drafted by the Pittsburgh Penguins in the 2026 NHL Draft this year. Their parents Derek and Nina Ruck reflect on raising the 18-year-old boys and how the draft went.

items.title: Taxpayers could be on hook for West Coast pipeline, but it's a 'good investment': Energy minister
items.bullet: Taxpayers could be on hook for West Coast pipeline, but it's a 'good investment': Energy minister (CBC Politics, Jul 3)
items.description: Energy Minister Tim Hodgson says a second pipeline to the West Coast will be money well-spent for the federal government but can't say how much Ottawa would be on the hook for.

items.title: Feds to weigh guardrails, alternatives to 'contentious' labour code tool: Hajdu
items.bullet: Feds to weigh guardrails, alternatives to 'contentious' labour code tool: Hajdu (CBC Politics, Jul 3)
items.description: Jobs Minister Patty Hajdu says the federal government is exploring possible guardrails or alternatives to using what she says is a "contentious" section of the Canada Labour Code that allows Ottawa to intervene in bitter bargaining conflicts.

items.title: Feeling sticker shock over Canadian beef? Here's why — and what's being done about it
items.bullet: Feeling sticker shock over Canadian beef? Here's why — and what's being done about it (CBC Politics, Jul 4)
items.description: Why do beef prices keep going up, and when can Canadians expect relief? CBC's The House spoke to experts across the Canadian meat industry about the ongoing issue and measures the federal government is pitching to address the problem.

items.title: Fans get home court advantage as first ever professional basketball game tips-off in Whitehorse
items.bullet: Fans get home court advantage as first ever professional basketball game tips-off in Whitehorse (CBC Top Stories, Jul 4)
items.description: The roar of a professional basketball crowd are sounds the Yukon had only heard on TV, until today. Basketball fans are excited following courtside access as the Canadian Elite Basketball League brings Whitehorse its first ever professional basketball game.

items.title: DND official rebuked for custom-made business card featuring F-35s
items.bullet: DND official rebuked for custom-made business card featuring F-35s (CBC Politics, Jul 4)
items.description: Amid the ongoing review of Canada's fighter jet procurement, a Department of National Defence official has been told to stop using a custom-made business card featuring two Lockheed Martin F-35s after she handed copies out at a recent military trade show.

items.title: Canada wants to build up to 10 new nuclear plants. Will our pension funds pay for them?
items.bullet: Canada wants to build up to 10 new nuclear plants. Will our pension funds pay for them? (CBC Top Stories, Jul 4)
items.description: Canada's ambitious new nuclear strategy has called for private investment to help expand the industry. Given that nuclear power plants are notoriously expensive to build, the government will have to heavily de-risk the ventures to attract large investors, like pension funds.

items.description: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

items.title: The Norway story - 28-year wait for their biggest-ever party
items.bullet: The Norway story - 28-year wait for their biggest-ever party (BBC Football, Jul 5)
items.description: Norway have waited 28 years for an appearance at the World Cup - now they face Brazil hoping to make history by reaching the quarter-finals for the first time.

items.title: Concerns raised about Jonathan Morgan prior to Maddy Cusack’s death, inquest told
items.bullet: Concerns raised about Jonathan Morgan prior to Maddy Cusack’s death, inquest told (The Guardian Football, Jul 3)
items.description: Former Sheffield United player Nina Wilson ‘tried to escalate concerns’ Morgan to give evidence to inquest on Monday The former Sheffield United goalkeeper Nina Wilson has told an inquest that she had “tried to escalate concerns” about the manager Jonathan Morgan prior to her teammate Maddy Cusack’s death in 2023, but she did not feel she was listened to. Wilson, who told the hearing that she had ended her own football career when she was aged 25 because of those experiences, said that neither she nor Cusack had known who the club’s safeguarding officer was, and she listed a series of recommendations to the court, including calling for whistleblowing routes to be clearer to players and for...

items.title: France find a way past Paraguay and what awaits England in Mexico? - World Cup Daily
items.bullet: France find a way past Paraguay and what awaits England in Mexico? - World Cup Daily (The Guardian Football, Jul 5)
items.description: Max Rushden is joined by Barry Glendenning , Archie Rhind-Tutt, Barney Ronay and Philippe Auclair as France beat a spiky Paraguay side to reach the quarter-finals Rate, review, share on Apple Podcasts and join the conversation on email . On the podcast today; a masterclass in the dark arts from Paraguay, who somehow didn’t receive a yellow card until after the game had finished. Ultimately a Kylian Mbappé penalty was enough to see France through to the quarter-finals where they will play Morocco. Continue reading...

items.title: Transfer rumors, news: Premier League clubs on ale...
items.bullet: Transfer rumors, news: Premier League clubs on ale... (ESPN Soccer, Jul 4)
items.description: Chelsea, Liverpool, Arsenal, Manchester City and Bayern Munich have been alerted over a potential deal for Barcelona defender Jules Kounde. Transfer Talk has the latest.

items.title: Morocco reach quarter-finals as co-hosts Canada knocked out
items.bullet: Morocco reach quarter-finals as co-hosts Canada knocked out (BBC Football, Jul 4)
items.description: Canada become the first of co-hosts to exit the 2026 World Cup, after being beaten 3-0 by Morocco in Houston.

items.title: ‘This is a hellhole’: Aramco makes its presence hurt in the shadow of the World Cup
items.bullet: ‘This is a hellhole’: Aramco makes its presence hurt in the shadow of the World Cup (The Guardian Football, Jul 4)
items.description: The oil giant’s sponsorship deal with Fifa has featured prominently at matches in Houston. But 100 miles away in another Texas city, residents say the firm’s refinery is exposing them to poisonous gases and long-term health problems The street is wide, its grass verges thick and scruffy after a week of rainstorms. Jamal Johnson will walk home straight down the middle carrying his plastic shopping bag, a jot of motion through the stillness. He lives in one of the modest wood-panelled houses spaced out on each side, most lovingly kept and passed through at least two generations. There is nobody else in sight, but a freight train breaks the silence, grinding left to right along the line...

items.title: 'I decided last minute!' - Salah on Panenka penalty

items.description: Mo Salah explains it was a last-minute decision to "Panenka" his penalty for Egypt after they beat Australia in a shootout to progress to the last 16 of the 2026 World Cup.
